chunks.content:
Article: TV deaths of Misato and Rits your initial reactions
[]: TV deaths of Misato and Rits - your initial reactions?             Quote Post by Maya Ibuki » Thu Sep 17, 2015 1:22 pm Though their deaths were explicitly shown in EoE, I was wondering what people initially thought of the glimpses provided in TV? Anyone a fan around that time care to comment? This, of course, only applies to those who watched TV before the feature films. It is ambiguous -- shots of Misato and Ritsuko dead on the floor - but we are never shown on TV as to who shot them. Do you think Anno at this stage thought that far ahead? Or were they just included to show the despair Shinji is describing - and not necessarily meant to be focused on per say? It explains the short glimpses. They are also left to interpretation as per Kaji's. Furthermore, do you think Anno came up with their EoE deaths as a way to shut fans up? Were how they died in EoE implied in EoTV? Was it his plan all along? There's no way whatsoever that I would've come to the conclusion that Ritsuko had been shot by Gendo without watching EoE... so just curious as to people's initial reactions.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Sep 17, 2015 1:33 pm I noted those fragments on the first viewing, and assumed that (following on from their conversation earlier), when Gendo had gone to start the HIP, they'd said "Over my dead body." and he was all "Happy to oblige."

[]:             Quote Post by Maya Ibuki » Thu Sep 17, 2015 1:36 pm So you initially thought Gendo shot Misato? Interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Sep 17, 2015 1:46 pm Given that Misato and Ritsuko seemed to have overcome earlier differences and were working together again, and given the lack of obvious other players in the game, who else could it have been?

[]:             Quote Post by Maya Ibuki » Thu Sep 17, 2015 1:51 pm Plot twist: Misato commits suicide. But it is interesting... now that you mention it I've just realised that we never see the JSSDF in EoTV... wondering whether or not that too was a last

Article: TV deaths of Misato and Rits your initial reactions
5 1:51 pm Plot twist: Misato commits suicide. But it is interesting... now that you mention it I've just realised that we never see the JSSDF in EoTV... wondering whether or not that too was a last minute touch by Anno?

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Thu Sep 17, 2015 1:55 pm Unlikely, if the notion that episode 25' was the original plan is correct

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 17, 2015 2:02 pm I was probably too confused by the shift from 24 to 25 to really think much of anything except, "What the hell?"

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Thu Sep 17, 2015 2:14 pm That depends. In the scenario where the NPC haven't been seen (or come out to home video yet), I guess the reaction would've been a major WTF. Especially for the viewers nationwide in 1996 Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Sep 17, 2015 3:31 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I noted those fragments on the first viewing, and assumed that (following on from their conversation earlier), when Gendo had gone to start the HIP, they'd said "Over my dead body." and he was all "Happy to oblige."

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Sep 17, 2015 3:36 pm I skipped the last two episodes and watched EOE right after the episode in which Kaworu died. So my reaction was like getting my heart ripped out. Moreso in Misato's case than Ritsuko's.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 17, 2015 3:52 pm NemZ wrote: Ritsuko apparently tried to run since she caught it in the back.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Sep 17, 2015 3:55 pm Mythbusters showed bullets break apart after roughly several feet of water. If Ritz dived and swam to the bottom she might have had a chance.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Sep 17, 2015 4:04 pm I didn't really know what to think since so much crazy stuff was going on

Article: TV deaths of Misato and Rits your initial reactions
 bottom she might have had a chance.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Sep 17, 2015 4:04 pm I didn't really know what to think since so much crazy stuff was going on, though I also (probably) assumed "Oh, Gendo shot them" since we cut to him talking shortly after.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Thu Sep 17, 2015 5:55 pm I thought they were metaphorical images representing some kind of emotion going on during instrumentality, or a hypothetical situation like the 26 AU. The barrage of imagery is so heavy that they don't immediately jump out as "oh, this is a thing that really happened."

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Sep 22, 2015 4:48 am I think I just linked those images with the rest of the death imagery associated with Instrumentality and the loss of self. EoE kinda cemented that association with death, so I don't think I was too far off, just confused as hell by everything else.

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Tue Sep 22, 2015 3:22 pm I remember seeing these episodes back in 1999 when ADV had released the show two episodes at a time on those weird white videotapes. I was like ten 10 (Completely unaware that EoE even existed) at the time, so I don't remember my specific reaction to seeing Ritusko and Misato dead, but I assumed something "cataclysmic" had occurred since the back of he VHS box ("Genesis 0:13" you guys know what I'm talking about?) said something about 3rd Impact somehow having occurred and also there was some quite from the book of Revelation that had been randomly been added on there that said something about the Lamb's book of life" and a figure "dressed in white." Plus it also referred to Shinji becoming the beast that shouted "I" at the world. I put these pieces together, from I understood about the Bible (and Antichrist/end times) at the time, along with the overall strangeness of what was happening to assume some kind of doomsday scenario/apocalypse was happening, but (obviously at that age) I didn't get the whole gist of it. I remember laughing quite a bit at the AU scenario.

Article: TV deaths of Misato and Rits your initial reactions
 overall strangeness of what was happening to assume some kind of doomsday scenario/apocalypse was happening, but (obviously at that age) I didn't get the whole gist of it. I remember laughing quite a bit at the AU scenario. I remember the parts the parts at the end about "everyone having their own truth" and thinking that sounded profound. I misunderstood the last shot, thinking they had all gone to a literal heaven. Although I only remember most of that so well, because my mom (who's crazy religious) got wind of what was going on (or at least pretended to understand) and began to declared "BLASPHEMY!" (mostly due to the VHS box) without any further discussion about it. Thus, opened a simultaneous life long obsession with Eva and rebellion against religion for me. As a side note, the first time I watched ep. 24, I recall yelling (and this something I totally feel bad about now) "Squish 'em! Squish 'em! Come on already, just squish him!"

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 22, 2015 6:59 pm SawItAtAge10 wrote: As a side note, the first time I watched ep. 24, I recall yelling (and this something I totally feel bad about now) "Squish 'em! Squish 'em! Come on already, just squish him!"

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Sep 22, 2015 7:21 pm When I saw Misato's and Ritsuko's bodies, I was struck with heaviness over death and sadness. It was just emotional, hopeless pain, really. That was about it.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Oct 08, 2015 3:59 am I've watched EoE before watching the TV series end. I wasn't really sad, I was just kinda overwhelmed by it. But yeah. Those shots of Asuka, Misato and Ritsuko in EoTV just underlines the fact that they already had a plan for Episode 25, but then didn't manage to realize them due to time and/or money.
